title: thebasics-handbook-title-languages
text: Languages can mark speech as Common, Tradeband, Sign, or server-defined languages. This system only appears when enabled by server config. Player language commands - /addlang <language>: learn a language when allowed. - /removelang <language>: remove a language when allowed. - /listlang: list your known languages. Speaking a language Use a prefix before your message, such as :c hello for Common or :tr hello for Tradeband. Using only the prefix, such as :c, changes your default speaking language. Unknown or unlearned languages are rejected. Sign language has its own range rules and may require line of sight. Chat accessibility Use /chatprefs or /chatprefs status to view your current settings. Use /chatprefs help for syntax. Common options include language colors, labels, nickname colors, and color presets. Useful accessibility commands - /chatprefs preset highcontrast - /chatprefs preset coloruniversal - /chatprefs preset protanopia - /chatprefs preset deuteranopia - /chatprefs preset tritanopia - /chatprefs preset monochrome - /langcolor [on|off]: shortcut for language color display. If a color or label makes chat harder to read, change it locally. These preferences affect your chat view; they do not change what other players see.
